title: Left Wheel Motor Error
shortDescription: The left drive wheel motor is stuck or not responding to the control board.
fullDescription:
Error code C01 on Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuums indicates that the left drive wheel motor is jammed, blocked, or has lost communication with the main control board. The robot relies on two independently driven wheels for navigation, and when the left wheel cannot rotate, the robot stops and displays C01.

The most common cause is hair, string, or debris wrapped around the left wheel axle, physically preventing it from turning. A malfunctioning wheel motor or a disconnected motor cable can also cause this error. In some cases, the robot may be stuck on an uneven surface where the wheel has lost traction and the motor stalls.

Start by turning the robot upside down and manually checking whether the left wheel spins freely. If it is jammed with debris, cleaning it usually resolves the issue immediately.

causes.cause: Hair or debris wrapped around the left wheel axle
Disconnected motor cable inside the robot
Robot stuck on an obstacle or uneven surface

symptoms: C01 error displayed on robot screen or in SmartThings app
Robot stops moving during cleaning
Robot spins in circles to the right
Left wheel does not rotate when lifted

fixSteps.title: Remove the robot from its location
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it on a flat, clear surface. Check if it was stuck on a rug edge, threshold, or cable.

fixSteps.title: Clean the left wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot upside down. Remove any hair, string, or debris wrapped around the left wheel and its axle. Use scissors to carefully cut away tangled hair.

fixSteps.title: Test wheel rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the left wheel by hand. It should rotate freely with slight resistance from the motor. If it is stiff or does not spin at all, the motor may be faulty.

fixSteps.title: Restart the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the power button for 10 seconds to force restart. Place the robot on the floor and start a new cleaning cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the wheel motor has failed and the wheel does not spin even after cleaning, the motor module needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C01 error fix

faq.q: How do I prevent C01 errors?
faq.a: Regularly check and clean both drive wheels. Remove hair and debris after each few cleaning sessions.

faq.q: Can I replace the wheel motor myself?
faq.a: It requires opening the robot chassis. If you are comfortable with small electronics, replacement modules are available, but warranty may be voided.

title: Right Wheel Motor Error
shortDescription: The right drive wheel motor is stuck or not responding to the control board.
fullDescription:
Error code C02 indicates the same issue as C01 but for the right wheel. The right drive wheel is jammed, blocked, or the motor has failed. The causes and fixes are identical to C01 but applied to the right side.

Hair and pet fur tangling around the wheel axle is the leading cause. Long carpet fibers or thin cables on the floor can also get caught in the wheel mechanism. The robot may display this error if it repeatedly fails to move the right wheel during a cleaning session.

Regular wheel maintenance — checking and cleaning both wheels every week for homes with pets — significantly reduces the frequency of C01 and C02 errors.

causes.cause: Hair or debris wrapped around the right wheel axle

symptoms: C02 error displayed on robot screen or in SmartThings app
Robot stops moving during cleaning
Robot spins in circles to the left
Right wheel does not rotate when lifted

fixSteps.title: Remove the robot from its position
fixSteps.instruction: Pick up the robot and place it on a flat surface.

fixSteps.title: Clean the right wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot upside down and remove hair, string, and debris from around the right wheel and axle. Use scissors to carefully cut away tangled material.

fixSteps.title: Test wheel rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the right wheel by hand. It should rotate smoothly. If stiff or locked, the motor may need replacement.

fixSteps.title: Restart the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 10 seconds to restart, then begin a new cleaning cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the right wheel motor is dead and cleaning does not help, contact Samsung support or a repair center for motor replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C02 error fix

faq.q: Is C02 the same as C01 but for the other wheel?
faq.a: Yes, C01 is the left wheel and C02 is the right wheel. The troubleshooting process is identical.

faq.q: My robot keeps getting C01 and C02 errors. What can I do?
faq.a: If you have pets, clean the wheels weekly. Also pick up cables, strings, and loose items from the floor before running the robot.

title: Main Brush Motor Error
shortDescription: The main brush roller motor is stuck or unable to rotate.
fullDescription:
Error code C03 indicates that the main brush (roller brush) motor is unable to spin. The main brush is the primary cleaning element that agitates dirt and debris from floors and carpets. When it cannot rotate, the robot stops the cleaning cycle.

The most frequent cause is hair, string, or large debris wrapped tightly around the brush roller, creating enough resistance to stall the motor. Over time, hair accumulation can become severe enough to burn out the motor if not cleaned regularly.

Samsung Jet Bot models have a removable brush assembly. Pulling out the brush and cutting away wrapped hair with scissors resolves this error in the vast majority of cases.

causes.cause: Hair and debris tangled around the main brush roller
Brush end cap jammed or cracked
Foreign object stuck between brush and housing

symptoms: C03 error on display or app
Robot stops cleaning
No sound of the brush spinning
Reduced cleaning performance before error appears

fixSteps.title: Remove the main brush
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot upside down. Unlock the brush cover by pressing the release tabs, then pull the brush roller out.

fixSteps.title: Clean the brush roller
fixSteps.instruction: Use scissors or a seam ripper to cut and remove all hair, string, and fibers wrapped around the brush. Clean both end caps and the brush housing.

fixSteps.title: Check the brush bearings
fixSteps.instruction: Spin the brush by hand. Both end caps should rotate smoothly. If one side is stiff or gritty, the end cap bearing may be worn and should be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall and test
fixSteps.instruction: Snap the brush back into place, secure the cover, and restart the robot.

whenToCallPro: If the motor itself has burned out (brush housing area smells burnt, motor does not engage even with a clean brush), professional repair or motor replacement is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C03 brush error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the main brush?
faq.a: At least once a week, or after every 2-3 cleaning sessions if you have pets with long hair.

faq.q: Can I wash the main brush with water?
faq.a: Yes, most Samsung Jet Bot brushes can be rinsed under water. Let it dry completely before reinstalling.

title: Side Brush Motor Error
shortDescription: The side brush motor is jammed or has failed to rotate.
fullDescription:
Error code C04 means the side brush motor cannot spin. The side brush extends from the robot's edge to sweep debris from corners and along walls into the path of the main brush. When it jams, the robot may display C04 and pause cleaning.

Hair wrapping around the side brush spindle is the primary cause. The side brush is a small spinning arm with bristles, and its motor is relatively low-torque, so even a small amount of tangled material can stall it.

Side brushes are designed to be user-replaceable and snap on and off easily.

causes.cause: Hair or debris tangled around side brush spindle
Cracked or deformed side brush arm

symptoms: C04 error on display or app
Side brush not spinning
Robot may still move but cleaning edges and corners is poor
Rattling noise from side brush area

fixSteps.title: Remove the side brush
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot upside down. The side brush snaps off — pull it straight out from the spindle.

fixSteps.title: Clean the spindle
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all hair and debris from the spindle shaft and the brush arm. Use tweezers for tightly wound material.

fixSteps.title: Check the brush for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the side brush arm for cracks or bent bristles. Replace if damaged — side brushes are inexpensive consumables.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall and test
fixSteps.instruction: Press the side brush back onto the spindle until it clicks. Restart the robot.

whenToCallPro: Only if the side brush motor itself is dead. Cleaning the spindle resolves this error in most cases.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C04 side brush error fix

faq.q: How often should I replace the side brush?
faq.a: Every 3–6 months depending on usage, or when the bristles are visibly worn or bent.

faq.q: Can the robot clean without a side brush?
faq.a: Yes, but edge and corner cleaning will be significantly reduced.

title: Cliff Sensor Error
shortDescription: One or more cliff (drop-off) sensors are blocked or malfunctioning.
fullDescription:
Error code C05 indicates that the cliff sensors on the bottom of the robot vacuum are obstructed or have failed. Cliff sensors use infrared light to detect drop-offs such as stairs. When they are blocked, the robot cannot safely navigate and stops as a safety precaution.

Dust, dirt, or debris covering the sensor windows is the most common cause. Dark-colored floors can sometimes confuse cliff sensors into thinking there is a drop-off, causing false C05 errors. A physically damaged sensor from a fall is less common but possible.

Cleaning the sensor windows with a soft dry cloth resolves this error in nearly all cases.

causes.cause: Dust or dirt covering cliff sensor windows
Robot operating on very dark or reflective floors
Damaged cliff sensor from a drop or impact

symptoms: C05 error on display or app
Robot refuses to move or immediately stops
Robot behaves as if it is always near a cliff
Robot backs up repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Clean the cliff sensors
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the robot upside down. Locate the 4-6 small infrared sensor windows along the front and side edges of the bottom. Wipe each one clean with a soft dry cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check the floor surface
fixSteps.instruction: If your floor is very dark (black marble, dark hardwood) or highly reflective, the cliff sensors may give false readings. Try running the robot on a lighter surface to confirm.

fixSteps.title: Restart the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 10 seconds to restart, then start a new cleaning cycle.

fixSteps.title: Test each sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Use the SmartThings app diagnostic mode (if available) or wave your hand under each sensor while the robot is on. The robot should react to each sensor.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the sensors does not resolve C05 and your floors are not extremely dark, a sensor may be physically damaged and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C05 cliff sensor error fix

faq.q: My robot gives C05 on my black floor. How can I fix this?
faq.a: Some users place white tape on the floor near stair edges instead of relying on cliff sensors. Some Jet Bot models allow disabling cliff sensors in the app for single-level homes.

faq.q: How many cliff sensors does the Jet Bot have?
faq.a: Most Samsung Jet Bot models have 4 to 6 cliff sensors on the bottom.

title: Bumper Sensor Stuck
shortDescription: The front bumper sensor is stuck in the pressed position and cannot return to its normal state.
fullDescription:
Error code C06 indicates that the front bumper sensor on the Samsung robot vacuum is stuck. The bumper is the curved front panel that physically contacts obstacles. It contains a sensor that detects collisions and tells the robot to change direction. If the bumper cannot return to its neutral position, the robot thinks it is perpetually hitting something.

Debris stuck between the bumper and the robot body is the most common cause. Small objects like a pen cap, crumb, or pet toy piece can wedge in the gap and prevent the bumper from springing back. A broken bumper spring or damaged sensor switch can also cause C06.

Tap the bumper firmly several times around its perimeter to dislodge any debris. If that works, inspect the gap for remaining material.

causes.cause: Debris wedged between bumper and robot body

symptoms: C06 error on display or app
Robot does not move forward
Bumper feels stuck when pressed
Robot repeatedly backs up without hitting anything

fixSteps.title: Tap the bumper
fixSteps.instruction: Firmly tap the bumper around its entire perimeter to dislodge any stuck debris. Press and release the bumper from different angles.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the bumper gap
fixSteps.instruction: Look into the gap between the bumper and the robot body. Use a flashlight to spot any wedged debris. Remove it with tweezers or compressed air.

fixSteps.title: Test bumper action
fixSteps.instruction: Press the bumper in and release. It should spring back immediately and evenly. If one section sticks, there may be internal debris or a broken spring.

fixSteps.title: Restart and test
fixSteps.instruction: Restart the robot and start a cleaning cycle. If C06 persists, the bumper assembly may need to be removed and cleaned internally.

whenToCallPro: If the bumper spring is broken or the sensor switch has failed, professional repair is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C06 bumper stuck error fix

faq.q: Can I clean inside the bumper myself?
faq.a: Some models allow removing the bumper cover with a few screws. Check your model's service manual. Be careful not to damage the sensor wires.

faq.q: Why does my robot keep hitting walls hard?
faq.a: This is a separate issue from C06. Hard wall collisions may indicate a bumper sensor that is not detecting contact early enough.

title: LiDAR Sensor Error
shortDescription: The LiDAR navigation sensor on top of the robot is obstructed or malfunctioning.
fullDescription:
Error code C07 indicates a problem with the LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) sensor turret on top of the robot. Samsung Jet Bot AI+ and similar models use a spinning LiDAR sensor to create a map of the room for intelligent navigation. If the sensor cannot spin or its readings are blocked, the robot cannot navigate.

The LiDAR turret sits in a raised dome on top of the robot. Dust accumulation on the sensor window, a jammed turret motor, or physical obstruction preventing the dome from spinning are the most common causes. Placing objects on top of the robot or operating it under very low furniture can also block the sensor.

Cleaning the LiDAR dome and ensuring it spins freely usually resolves C07.

causes.cause: Dust or smudges on LiDAR sensor window

symptoms: C07 error on display or app
Robot cannot map the room
Robot moves randomly instead of systematically
Robot refuses to start cleaning

fixSteps.title: Clean the LiDAR dome
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the entire LiDAR turret dome on top of the robot with a soft dry cloth. Remove any dust, fingerprints, or smudges.

fixSteps.title: Check turret rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Gently try to rotate the LiDAR dome by hand. It should spin freely with minimal resistance. If it is stuck, check for debris at the base of the dome.

fixSteps.title: Remove obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is placed on top of the robot and that it is not trying to clean under furniture that is too low for the LiDAR dome to clear.

fixSteps.title: Restart the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Power off the robot completely, wait 10 seconds, then power it back on and start a new cleaning session.

whenToCallPro: If the LiDAR turret motor has failed or the sensor hardware is damaged, professional repair is required.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C07 LiDAR sensor error fix

faq.q: Do all Samsung robot vacuums have LiDAR?
faq.a: No. Only higher-end models like the Jet Bot AI+ use LiDAR. Basic models use camera-based or gyroscope navigation.

faq.q: Can I run the robot without LiDAR?
faq.a: No. If the LiDAR sensor is not functioning, the robot will not clean. It requires the sensor for navigation.

title: Dustbin Not Detected
shortDescription: The robot cannot detect the dustbin. It may be missing, improperly seated, or the detection sensor is faulty.
fullDescription:
Error code C08 means the robot vacuum cannot detect its dustbin. Samsung Jet Bot models have a sensor that confirms the dustbin is properly installed before starting a cleaning cycle. If the bin is missing, not fully inserted, or the sensor is blocked, the robot displays C08 and refuses to clean.

The dustbin must click into place securely. Dirt on the bin contacts or sensor area can prevent detection. After emptying the bin, users sometimes do not push it all the way back in.

This is a simple mechanical issue in almost all cases — reinstalling the dustbin firmly resolves C08.

causes.cause: Dirty contacts on bin or robot body

symptoms: C08 error on display or app
Robot refuses to start cleaning
Error appears after emptying the dustbin

fixSteps.title: Remove and reinstall the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Press the dustbin release button, pull the bin out completely, and then push it back in until you hear a firm click.

fixSteps.title: Clean the contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the metal contacts on both the dustbin and the robot body with a dry cloth. Remove any dust or debris from the sensor area.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Check the bin for cracks or warping that could prevent it from seating properly. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Restart the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Power cycle the robot and attempt a new cleaning session.

whenToCallPro: Only if the detection sensor inside the robot is faulty, which is rare.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C08 dustbin error fix

faq.q: Can I use the robot without a dustbin?
faq.a: No. The robot will not start cleaning without a properly detected dustbin.

faq.q: Where can I buy a replacement dustbin?
faq.a: Samsung parts stores, Amazon, and authorized dealers carry replacement dustbins for Jet Bot models.

title: Battery Error
shortDescription: The robot has detected a battery malfunction — the battery is not charging, overheating, or providing incorrect voltage.
fullDescription:
Error code C09 indicates a battery-related issue with the Samsung robot vacuum. The control board monitors battery voltage, temperature, and charge state. When any of these parameters are out of range, C09 is displayed.

A degraded battery that can no longer hold charge is the most common cause in older units. A faulty charging station, damaged charging contacts on the robot, or a battery that has overheated can also trigger C09. If the robot was stored for a long period without charging, the battery may have discharged below its recovery threshold.

Batteries are consumable components and typically last 2–3 years with regular use before needing replacement.

causes.cause: Degraded battery at end of lifespan
Dirty charging contacts on robot or station
Battery overheating due to defective cells

symptoms: C09 error on display or app
Robot does not charge or charges very slowly
Robot runs for only a few minutes before dying
Battery indicator flashes abnormally

fixSteps.title: Clean charging contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the charging contacts on both the robot's underside and the charging station with a dry cloth to ensure good electrical contact.

fixSteps.title: Try a different outlet
fixSteps.instruction: Plug the charging station into a different wall outlet to rule out a power supply issue.

fixSteps.title: Reset the robot
fixSteps.instruction: Hold the power button for 15 seconds to perform a hard reset. Place the robot back on the charging station and wait 30 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Replace the battery
fixSteps.instruction: If the robot is over 2 years old and C09 persists, the battery likely needs replacement. Remove the bottom cover screws, disconnect the old battery, and install a Samsung-compatible replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the battery is swollen or has leaked, do not attempt to remove it yourself. Contact Samsung support for safe disposal and replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C09 battery error fix

faq.q: How long does a Samsung robot vacuum battery last?
faq.a: Typically 2–3 years with regular use. Battery life gradually decreases as the cells degrade.

faq.q: Can I use a third-party battery?
faq.a: Third-party batteries are available and often cheaper, but ensure they match the voltage and connector. Using non-OEM batteries may void the warranty.

title: Suction Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The suction fan motor is not operating or is running below the expected speed.
fullDescription:
Error code C10 indicates that the suction fan (vacuum motor) inside the robot is not functioning correctly. The suction fan creates the airflow that pulls dirt and debris from the floor through the main brush and into the dustbin. Without proper suction, the robot cannot clean effectively.

A clogged dustbin or filter is the most common cause — when airflow is severely restricted, the fan motor cannot reach its target RPM and the control board generates C10. A blockage in the suction pathway (between the brush and the bin) can have the same effect. Less commonly, the fan motor itself may have failed.

Always check the dustbin and filter first before suspecting a motor problem.
symptoms: C10 error on display or app
Robot runs but does not pick up debris
Reduced or no suction sound
Robot stops mid-cleaning

fixSteps.title: Empty the dustbin
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and empty the dustbin completely. Check for compacted debris that may be blocking the bin opening.

fixSteps.title: Clean or replace the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the filter from the dustbin. Tap it over a trash can to remove loose dust. If it is visibly clogged, rinse it under water (if washable) and let it dry completely for 24 hours. Replace if it is damaged or very old.

fixSteps.title: Check the suction pathway
fixSteps.instruction: Look into the suction opening on the robot (where the dustbin connects to the brush area). Remove any large debris or blockages with tweezers.

fixSteps.title: Restart and test
fixSteps.instruction: Reinstall the clean bin and filter, restart the robot, and listen for normal suction sound during operation.

whenToCallPro: If emptying the bin, cleaning the filter, and clearing blockages does not resolve C10, the suction motor may have failed and needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung Jet Bot robot vacuum C10 suction error fix

faq.q: How often should I replace the filter?
faq.a: Samsung recommends replacing the filter every 2–3 months, or sooner if you have pets or clean frequently.

faq.q: Can a full dustbin damage the suction motor?
faq.a: Running with a consistently full bin forces the motor to work harder against restricted airflow, which can shorten its lifespan over time.
